items.prompt: A design review covers Delimiter types and mixed nesting. Classify balanced-delimiter validation as an ordinary LIFO use. Which statement gives the decisive justification?

options.text: Nesting closes the most recent unmatched opener; value order is irrelevant.
Any use of a stack with comparisons is monotonic.
The variant is determined by whether entries are numeric or indices; their semantic role and the requested output do not matter.
Use a globally sorted priority structure whenever values appear, because encounter order and provenance are never part of correctness.

feedback.reason: Nesting closes the most recent unmatched opener; value order is irrelevant. This is the decisive boundary for the stated output and retained-state semantics.
feedback.details: The item is decided by retained-state meaning and whether ordered unresolved candidates are required, not by a surface feature. Nesting closes the most recent unmatched opener; value order is irrelevant. The tempting claim—Any use of a stack with comparisons is monotonic.—fails because it does not establish the semantic invariant that makes removal or retention safe. Transfer when the retained entry changes from recent pending state to an ordered unresolved candidate, or vice versa; re-derive whether value order and safe dominance removal are required.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.target_false_heuristic: This is the targeted misconception. Nesting closes the most recent unmatched opener; value order is irrelevant. The quoted clue does not establish that invariant or justify the removal rule.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.surface_feature_classifier: This relies on a surface feature instead of the contract. The correct decision follows from retained-state meaning and whether ordered unresolved candidates are required; payload type or implementation shape alone is not diagnostic.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.unjustified_alternative_rule: This changes the representation or transition without proving that it preserves the requested result. Nesting closes the most recent unmatched opener; value order is irrelevant.
